Given our initial distribution from the model for a next token, given our input, we can do two kind of different things.
We can add each individual decoding step, choose some kind of function to manipulate this distribution.
And this could be something like short like cutting off the long tail, like modifying the temperature or adding extra information from another model or from a discriminator model.
Or we can over a larger part of the decoding process, choose some function to choose between sequences.
And this could be like choosing between next token and beam search from what we're putting beams.
This could be choosing from full sequences when we're doing something like MBR or sample and rerank methods.
And you can do these two things in parallel, right?
So we can use a different function to manipulate next token distribution and then some sort of like broader thing to choose what you do with the full sequences you get out of that distribution.
But there are sort of these two broad categories of decoding.
